enonce.texte: Match the modal verbs (1-6) with their correct functions (A-F).

questions.texte: must
questions.reponse: A

questions.texte: have to
questions.reponse: E

questions.texte: should
questions.reponse: B

questions.texte: ought to
questions.reponse: F

questions.texte: don't have to
questions.reponse: C

questions.texte: shouldn't
questions.reponse: D

intitule: Using Must, Have to, Should, and Ought to

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct modal verb: must, have to, should, ought to, don't have to, shouldn't.

questions.texte: You ________ wear a seatbelt when you drive. It's the law.
questions.reponse: must / have to

questions.texte: I ________ finish this report today because my boss needs it.
questions.reponse: have to

questions.texte: You ________ visit the dentist. Your teeth look very yellow.
questions.reponse: should / ought to

questions.texte: Students ________ bring their books to class every day.
questions.reponse: must / have to

questions.texte: We ________ go to work on Sunday. It's our day off.
questions.reponse: don't have to

questions.texte: You ________ smoke in public places. It's forbidden.
questions.reponse: mustn't / shouldn't

questions.texte: She ________ apologise to her friend. She was wrong.
questions.reponse: should / ought to

questions.texte: We ________ pay the school fees by the end of the month.
questions.reponse: have to / must

enonce.texte: Read each sentence and decide whether the obligation is personal (internal, moral, or from the speaker's authority) or external (from a law, rule, or another person). Then, identify the modal verb used.

questions.texte: I must stop eating so much sugar. It's bad for my health.
questions.reponse: Personal obligation (internal). The speaker feels this obligation for their own health.

questions.texte: In Cameroon, drivers have to carry their driving license at all times.
questions.reponse: External obligation (law). It is required by law.

questions.texte: You must respect your elders. It's part of our culture.
questions.reponse: Personal obligation (moral/cultural). It comes from cultural values.

questions.texte: Employees have to arrive at work by 8:00 AM according to company policy.
questions.reponse: External obligation (company policy). The rule is imposed by the company.

questions.texte: I really should call my grandmother. I haven't spoken to her in weeks.
questions.reponse: Personal obligation (internal). The speaker feels they should do it for personal reasons.

intitule: Applying Obligation and Advice in Real-Life Contexts

enonce.texte: Read the following scenarios and use the correct modal verbs to answer the questions.

questions.texte: Your friend looks very tired and has been coughing all day. What advice do you give him/her?
questions.reponse: You should see a doctor. / You ought to see a doctor.

questions.texte: Your brother wants to buy a new phone but he doesn't have enough money. What advice do you give him?
questions.reponse: You should save some money first. / You shouldn't buy it until you have enough money.

questions.texte: You are at a school where students are required to wear a uniform. What does this rule mean for the students?
questions.reponse: Students have to wear a uniform. / Students must wear a uniform.

questions.texte: You feel it is important to be honest with people. What does this personal belief mean for you?
questions.reponse: I must always tell the truth. / I should be honest with people.

questions.texte: You are invited to a party but you have an exam the next day. What should you do?
questions.reponse: I shouldn't go to the party. / I should stay home and study.

intitule: Understanding Obligation and Advice in Cameroonian Contexts

enonce.texte:
Read the following text about life in Cameroon and answer the questions using modal verbs of obligation and advice.

"In Cameroon, students have to attend school regularly to succeed. Parents must pay school fees on time. However, children should also help their parents at home. In many families, children ought to respect their elders and follow traditional values. If you want to succeed in your studies, you must study hard and avoid bad company. You don't have to stay at school after classes if you don't have any activities, but you should use your free time wisely. Students shouldn't waste time on social media instead of doing their homework."

questions.texte: According to the text, what obligation do students have for their success?
questions.reponse: Students have to attend school regularly.

questions.texte: What should children do at home according to the text?
questions.reponse: Children should help their parents at home.

questions.texte: What advice does the text give about studying?
questions.reponse: You must study hard and avoid bad company. You should use your free time wisely.

questions.texte: What is NOT required of students after classes?
questions.reponse: Students don't have to stay at school after classes if they don't have any activities.

questions.texte: What shouldn't students waste time on?
questions.reponse: Students shouldn't waste time on social media instead of doing their homework.

intitule: Writing Advice and Obligations for New Students

enonce.texte: Imagine you are a senior student in a Cameroonian secondary school. Write a short guide (5-7 sentences) for new students. Give them advice and explain what they must, have to, should, and shouldn't do. Use at least five of the following modal verbs: must, have to, should, ought to, don't have to, shouldn't.

questions.texte: Write your guide for new students here.
questions.reponse: Welcome to our school! First of all, you must respect the school rules and the teachers. You have to wear the school uniform every day. You should arrive on time for classes because punctuality is important. You ought to participate in class activities to learn better. You don't have to bring expensive items to school, but you should bring your books and stationery. Finally, you shouldn't stay out late during school days. Good luck with your studies!
